title: The Zero-Sum Game
summary: Tzubaki is caught eating illegal phase-shifted berries in the forest. Brought to the Academy in chains. Put in a cage with a captured Harvester to prove his worth. Uses Ghost Heart to ignore gravity, un-peels the stolen tattoo with one touch. Headmistress realizes his potential and offers the Scholarship/Debt deal.

panels.description: Wide shot of the ancient forest. Dappled sunlight through massive trees, overgrown roots, mystical atmosphere. Sets the scene — wild, untamed, full of secrets.
Tzubaki sitting cross-legged in a clearing, casually eating glowing purple berries. Messy black hair, red eyes, black robe. Completely unbothered, like he's having a picnic.

dialogue.text: Phase-shifted berries. Illegal in seven provinces. ...Honestly? Worth it.

panels.description: Close-up of Tzubaki's face mid-chew, eyes suddenly wide. He's noticed something. The carefree expression shifts to alert in a heartbeat.

Tzubaki being dragged through the grand Academy halls in chains. Marble floors, stained glass, golden light everywhere. He looks completely out of place — and completely unimpressed. Guards on either side.

dialogue.text: Nice floors. Do you guys polish these daily, or is this a special occasion?

panels.description: The underground cage arena. Chain-link walls, concrete floor, harsh spotlights from above. Industrial, brutal. This is where they test 'dangerous' individuals.
Over Tzubaki's shoulder, looking at the captured Harvester — a hulking figure with crude armor and a glowing stolen Golden Script on his arm. The Harvester is snarling, ready to kill.
Close-up of Tzubaki's face. Calm. Analytical. His eyes are scanning the Harvester's stolen script like he's reading code. The faintest smirk.

dialogue.text: Nice script. Stolen, obviously. The fracture line on the third ring? Dead giveaway. ...Also, your left side is completely open.

panels.description: The Harvester lunges with a crushing gravity-field attack. Tzubaki's ink mark shifts to Ghost Heart — his body becomes grey smoke. The gravity passes through him. He walks forward through the attack, completely unaffected.

dialogue.text: Gravity field. Cute. Gonna cost you extra though.

panels.description: Extreme close-up of Tzubaki's hand touching the Harvester's stolen tattoo. The ink on his hand shifts to Flowing Heart — rippling, liquid. The stolen Golden Script begins to dissolve, peeling off the Harvester's skin like wet paint.

Tzubaki standing over the defeated Harvester, holding a small collection jar with a dissolved Golden Script swirling inside. He's dusting off his hands like he just finished a chore. Guards stare in shock.

dialogue.text: One un-peeled tattoo. Could've done it faster, but the dramatic pause felt right.

panels.description: The Headmistress steps forward from the observation deck. Silver hair in a bun, glasses catching the light, gold-trimmed robes. Her expression is pure calculation — she's not impressed, she's measuring.
Profile shot of the Headmistress speaking. Sharp eyes, calculating smile. She's looking down at Tzubaki like a chess piece she just figured out how to use.

dialogue.text: Living Ink. Unregistered. Unbound. ...And you just proved you can extract what our best surgeons cannot.

panels.description: Close-up of Tzubaki's face. The carefree mask slips for just a second — his red eyes narrow. He knows exactly what's coming: a deal he can't refuse.

dialogue.text: ...This is the part where you name a price, isn't it.

panels.description: Split composition — Headmistress on one side, Tzubaki on the other. She's offering a scroll (the contract). He's looking at it like a bill he didn't order. The deal is being struck.

dialogue.text: Full scholarship. Room, board, training. In exchange... you hunt every Harvester we can't catch.

panels.description: Final shot. Tzubaki's face — a slow grin spreading. Not happy. Not excited. The grin of someone who just calculated the odds and found them... acceptable.

dialogue.text: So I eat for free, and I get to punch people who deserve it. ...Where do I sign?

notes: Episode 1 establishes Tzubaki's personality (carefree but sharp), his magic system (Three Hearts), and the central conflict (the Debt deal). The Headmistress is introduced as calculating authority, not a friend. Ends on a hook — the deal is accepted, but the real cost is unknown.
